title: Review of Museum of History & Industry (MOHAI) | Seattle, Washington, North America - AFAR
content: December 29th 2012, Opening Day at the new South Lake Union location of MOHAI. Better known as MOHAI, this collection dives deep into local history, from the region’s maritime history to its tradition of technological innovation. Highlights include Boeing’s first commercial plane, the 1856 Petticoat Flag sewn by women during the Battle of Seattle, and the original Rainier Brewing Company neon *R* sign. Seattle’s Museum of History and Industry moved to swanky new digs on Lake Union in 2013, making it a must-see for local history enthusiasts. The permanent collection includes an interactive historical narrative of Seattle with plenty of art and artifacts that bring the past to life, an exhibit on maritime Seattle, and the Bezos Center for Innovation, celebrating Washington’s tech innovators and inventors. Highlights of the permanent collection include Boeing’s first commercial plane, the tiny 1919 Boeing B-1; the “Petticoat Flag,” an 1856 American flag sewn by women during the Battle of Seattle; and the Rainier Brewing Company’s iconic 12-foot tall neon “R” sign.

title: Review of Smith Tower Visitor Experience, Seattle, WA - Tripadvisor
content: Aidan (and Richard) were great tour guides! So much history in that tower! They kept it fun and informative, and there’s a great ending at the bar upstairs. ### Smith Tower Observatory Admission Ticket. ### Seattle City and Snoqualmie Falls Half-Day Guided Tour. The hours on the ticket were incorrect. We look forward to hosting you on site here at the historic Smith Tower. It was worth getting the tour and not just doing it on your own unless you just go for the view. We enjoyed our bar drinks and viewings of the city and mountain ranges. It was a perfect day and we are so happy you joined us. Our staff in the Observatory are so happy to have served you and we are so excited to host you once more here at the historic Smith Tower. We look forward to you visiting again, and including you in our history here at the Smith Tower.
